p.seg: To all sons of Holy Mother Church present and future, Patrick of Ryedale sends greeting.

Know me to have licensed and by this my charter to have made firm to God and to the Church of St Mary at Melrose and to the monks serving God in that place the donation that Geoffrey son of Waldef of Lilliesleaf which he made to the aforesaid church and monks in respect of three oxgangs of arable land in the territory of Whitton, in free, pure and perpetual alms, just as they lie in one holding above ‘Raven’s Fen’ near to the land of ‘Heuiside’ which they have from my donation. Wherefore I will that the aforesaid monks of Melrose should hold and possess the aforenamed land free, quit, and loosed from every earthly service and custom and secular exaction just as the charter of the aforesaid Geoffrey more fully bears witness and affirms. And I and my heirs shall maintain and warrant to the oft-mentioned monks the aforewritten land against all men for ever; saving only the service to me and my heirs from Geoffrey himself and his heirs in respect of the same land.
